lines.line: # Introduction to Elsie Shaw Tulloch

> The speaker introduces herself as Hannah Laurie Hine, a state historian, and begins an interview with Elsie Shaw Tulloch, an administrator for the Idaho Division of Public Health. The interview is part of a Covid-19 oral history project. Elsie spells out her name and expresses her interest in the conversation. She explains her educational background in environmental health and health science, which was sparked by her interest in how society interacts with the environment and the desire to protect it.

metadata.title: Introduction to Elsie Shaw Tulloch
metadata.synopsis: The speaker introduces herself as Hannah Laurie Hine, a state historian, and begins an interview with Elsie Shaw Tulloch, an administrator for the Idaho Division of Public Health. The interview is part of a Covid-19 oral history project. Elsie spells out her name and expresses her interest in the conversation. She explains her educational background in environmental health and health science, which was sparked by her interest in how society interacts with the environment and the desire to protect it.

lines.line: # Defining Public Health

> The speaker provides her personal definition of public health, emphasizing the importance of creating environments where people can live healthy lives free from disease. She highlights the role of prevention and regulatory measures in public health and the need for interventions that make healthy choices the easy choice for the public.

metadata.title: Defining Public Health
metadata.synopsis: The speaker provides her personal definition of public health, emphasizing the importance of creating environments where people can live healthy lives free from disease. She highlights the role of prevention and regulatory measures in public health and the need for interventions that make healthy choices the easy choice for the public.

lines.line: # Elsie's Role as Administrator

> Elsie Shaw Tulloch discusses her career progression within the Department of Health and Welfare, starting from her initial role in 1996 to her current position as the administrator of the Idaho Division of Public Health. She describes her various roles and the evolution of her responsibilities, leading to her becoming the longest-standing state health official in the US and territories.

metadata.title: Elsie's Role as Administrator
metadata.synopsis: Elsie Shaw Tulloch discusses her career progression within the Department of Health and Welfare, starting from her initial role in 1996 to her current position as the administrator of the Idaho Division of Public Health. She describes her various roles and the evolution of her responsibilities, leading to her becoming the longest-standing state health official in the US and territories.

lines.line: # Pandemic's Impact on Other Health Concerns

> The speaker acknowledges the challenges of maintaining other public health services during the pandemic. She notes that while the focus was on Covid-19, the division still managed to support its staff and continue its operations, although some staff were reassigned to pandemic response roles.

metadata.title: Pandemic's Impact on Other Health Concerns
metadata.synopsis: The speaker acknowledges the challenges of maintaining other public health services during the pandemic. She notes that while the focus was on Covid-19, the division still managed to support its staff and continue its operations, although some staff were reassigned to pandemic response roles.

lines.line: # Overview of Division's Programs and Services

> The speaker provides a detailed overview of the eight bureaus within her division and the wide range of prevention activities, clinical services, and technical services they offer. She explains the division's focus on areas such as women's health, chronic disease, emergency medical services, communicable disease prevention, and laboratory services. The division also includes a new bureau focused on equity and strategic partnerships.

metadata.title: Overview of Division's Programs and Services
metadata.synopsis: The speaker provides a detailed overview of the eight bureaus within her division and the wide range of prevention activities, clinical services, and technical services they offer. She explains the division's focus on areas such as women's health, chronic disease, emergency medical services, communicable disease prevention, and laboratory services. The division also includes a new bureau focused on equity and strategic partnerships.

lines.line: # Initial Response to Covid-19 Pandemic

> The speaker recounts the early days of the Covid-19 pandemic, describing the heightened alert and anxiety as the virus spread from China to the US. She details the establishment of an incident command structure, the challenges of monitoring and testing, and the relief felt when the first positive case was confirmed in Idaho, allowing for a more directed response.

metadata.title: Initial Response to Covid-19 Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts the early days of the Covid-19 pandemic, describing the heightened alert and anxiety as the virus spread from China to the US. She details the establishment of an incident command structure, the challenges of monitoring and testing, and the relief felt when the first positive case was confirmed in Idaho, allowing for a more directed response.

lines.line: # Stay-at-Home Order and Rebounds Plan

> The speaker describes the period between the first confirmed case and the stay-at-home order, highlighting the intense workload and the establishment of the Idaho Rebounds plan. She discusses her involvement in formulating the plan, particularly the stages of reopening and the balance between public health recommendations and economic considerations.

metadata.title: Stay-at-Home Order and Rebounds Plan
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes the period between the first confirmed case and the stay-at-home order, highlighting the intense workload and the establishment of the Idaho Rebounds plan. She discusses her involvement in formulating the plan, particularly the stages of reopening and the balance between public health recommendations and economic considerations.

lines.line: # Communication During the Pandemic

> The speaker discusses the various communication strategies used during the pandemic, including Facebook Live Q&A sessions and press briefings. She emphasizes the importance of these platforms in providing real-time information and addressing public concerns, despite challenges such as misinformation and the need for a comprehensive communication campaign.

metadata.title: Communication During the Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the various communication strategies used during the pandemic, including Facebook Live Q&A sessions and press briefings. She emphasizes the importance of these platforms in providing real-time information and addressing public concerns, despite challenges such as misinformation and the need for a comprehensive communication campaign.

lines.line: # Data Collection and Reporting

> The speaker reflects on the importance of data collection and the challenges of reporting accurate Covid-19 statistics. She explains the use of dashboards to display case numbers and testing volumes, and the decision to continue reporting Covid-related deaths even after the end of the national public health emergency. The speaker emphasizes the human aspect behind the data, sharing her personal practice of reviewing each death certificate to honor the individuals who passed away.

metadata.title: Data Collection and Reporting
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the importance of data collection and the challenges of reporting accurate Covid-19 statistics. She explains the use of dashboards to display case numbers and testing volumes, and the decision to continue reporting Covid-related deaths even after the end of the national public health emergency. The speaker emphasizes the human aspect behind the data, sharing her personal practice of reviewing each death certificate to honor the individuals who passed away.

lines.line: # Vaccination Rollout and Hesitancy

> The speaker addresses the vaccination rollout in Idaho, discussing the various reasons for public hesitancy, including fear, misinformation, and the complexity of the vaccination schedule. She notes the success of vaccination events, especially in rural areas, where trust and accessibility were key factors.

metadata.title: Vaccination Rollout and Hesitancy
metadata.synopsis: The speaker addresses the vaccination rollout in Idaho, discussing the various reasons for public hesitancy, including fear, misinformation, and the complexity of the vaccination schedule. She notes the success of vaccination events, especially in rural areas, where trust and accessibility were key factors.

lines.line: # Leadership Reflections and Personal Impact

> The speaker reflects on her leadership during the pandemic, the importance of supporting her team, and the personal impact of the crisis on her family life. She discusses the challenges of balancing work with family responsibilities and the collective trauma experienced by her staff and herself.

metadata.title: Leadership Reflections and Personal Impact
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on her leadership during the pandemic, the importance of supporting her team, and the personal impact of the crisis on her family life. She discusses the challenges of balancing work with family responsibilities and the collective trauma experienced by her staff and herself.

lines.line: # Agency Perception and Future Outlook

> The speaker evaluates the response of the Department of Health and Welfare to the pandemic, expressing pride in the agency's significant impact and the balance achieved between health measures and economic considerations. She discusses the current perception of the agency, which includes both recognition and political backlash, and the challenges posed by recent policy changes.

> The speaker became the administrator of the Idaho Division of Public Health in 2012. She describes her journey within the department, starting from her initial role in 1996, through various positions, to her current role as administrator. She also mentions her responsibilities, including supporting her staff and ensuring the division meets its strategic goals.

lines.line: # Personal Reflections and Impact on Family

> The speaker provides personal reflections on the impact of the pandemic on her family and personal life. She discusses the challenges of balancing her responsibilities with the needs of her family, the sense of isolation, and the importance of self-care during this unprecedented time.

lines.line: # Overview of Division's Core Programs and Services

> The speaker provides a comprehensive overview of the eight bureaus within the Division of Public Health and the various programs and services they manage. These range from clinical and preventive services to emergency medical services and preparedness, highlighting the division's focus on prevention and support for healthy living across Idaho.

lines.line: # Stay-at-Home Order and Subsequent Actions

> The speaker describes the period between the first confirmed case of Covid-19 and the issuance of the stay-at-home order by Governor Little. She discusses the intense work and decision-making process during this time, including the establishment of the Idaho Rebounds plan and the challenges of balancing public health with economic considerations.

lines.line: # Perception of the Department and Lessons Learned

Despite these difficulties, she highlights the department's achievements in keeping Idahoans safe and supporting the state's health infrastructure.

children.text: # Introduction to the Oral History Interview

> The speaker introduces herself as Hannah Laurie Hine, a state historian for the Idaho State Historical Society, and begins an oral history interview with Elsie Shaw Tulloch, an administrator for the Idaho Division of Public Health. The interview is part of a larger Covid-19 oral history project, and the speaker outlines the structure and purpose of the conversation.
# Initial Response to Covid-19

> The speaker recounts the initial response to Covid-19, including the heightened alert due to the situation in China, the establishment of an incident command structure, and the anticipation of the first positive case in Idaho. The confirmation of the first case brought a sense of relief as it allowed the state to move forward with its response.
# Elsie Shaw Tulloch's Career Path and Public Health Definition

> Elsie Shaw Tulloch discusses her educational background and the reasons behind her career choice in environmental health and health science. She provides her personal definition of public health, emphasizing the importance of creating environments where people can lead healthy lives free from disease.
# Roles Before and As Administrator

> The speaker became the administrator of the Idaho Division of Public Health in 2012. She describes her journey within the department, starting from her initial role in 1996, through various positions, to her current role as administrator. She also mentions her responsibilities, including supporting her staff and ensuring the division meets its strategic goals.
# Personal Reflections and Impact on Family

> The speaker provides personal reflections on the impact of the pandemic on her family and personal life. She discusses the challenges of balancing her responsibilities with the needs of her family, the sense of isolation, and the importance of self-care during this unprecedented time.
# Overview of Division's Core Programs and Services

> The speaker provides a comprehensive overview of the eight bureaus within the Division of Public Health and the various programs and services they manage. These range from clinical and preventive services to emergency medical services and preparedness, highlighting the division's focus on prevention and support for healthy living across Idaho.
# Stay-at-Home Order and Subsequent Actions

> The speaker describes the period between the first confirmed case of Covid-19 and the issuance of the stay-at-home order by Governor Little. She discusses the intense work and decision-making process during this time, including the establishment of the Idaho Rebounds plan and the challenges of balancing public health with economic considerations.
# Managing Division Workload and Staff During Pandemic

> The speaker discusses the challenges of managing the division's workload and staff during the early weeks of the pandemic. She describes how the division had to adapt to the increased demands, with staff taking on additional responsibilities and the need to support each other while continuing to provide essential services.
# Communication Strategies During the Pandemic

> The speaker talks about the various communication strategies used during the pandemic, including Facebook Live Q&A sessions and press briefings. She emphasizes the importance of these platforms in providing real-time information and addressing public concerns, despite the challenges of misinformation and the need for constant communication.
# Data Collection and Reporting

> The speaker addresses the critical role of data collection and reporting during the pandemic, discussing the challenges and importance of accurate and timely data. She reflects on the emotional impact of reviewing death certificates and the significance of each life lost to Covid-19, underscoring the human aspect behind the data.
# Vaccination Rollout and Hesitancy

> The speaker reflects on the vaccination rollout in Idaho, discussing the factors contributing to vaccine hesitancy, such as fear, misinformation, and the complexity of the vaccination schedule. She notes the success of vaccination events, especially in rural areas, where trust and accessibility played key roles in encouraging people to get vaccinated.
# Perception of the Department and Lessons Learned

> The speaker discusses the perception of the Department of Health and Welfare and the lessons learned from the pandemic. She expresses pride in the department's significant role in responding to the crisis and the challenges faced, including political and policy backlash. Despite these difficulties, she highlights the department's achievements in keeping Idahoans safe and supporting the state's health infrastructure.

story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Elke Shaw Tulloch
story.description: The interview focuses on Elke Shaw-Tullock's experiences and actions as the Administrator for the Idaho Division of Public Health during the COVID-19 pandemic. Key topics discussed include her career path, the comprehensive responsibilities of the division, and the public health strategies employed in response to the pandemic. Elke details the efforts made to maintain transparency and public communication through methods like Facebook Live Q&A sessions and media briefings. She addresses challenges such as public misinformation, political dynamics, and vaccination hesitancy. Additionally, Elke reflects on the emotional weight of tracking COVID-19 deaths and the overall impact of the pandemic on her professional and personal life, emphasizing the importance of teamwork and leadership.
